name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: Day 76. **Henry Hub $2.89/MMBtu (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage; Cheniere supply pricing pulling US gas higher (~80% crisis). **TTF €46.63/MWh May 13 (-0.11% 1D, basically flat at +47% from pre-war €31.20)** — sustained-elevated mode, neither alarm nor relief (B4). **C3 Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = $3.5bn Q1 hedging derivatives loss from Iran-war price blowout + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on diminishing Hormuz-tail-risk + Trump-Xi opening = US LNG-to-China future demand normalization (positive long-term but reduces crisis-premium short-term). **The Q1 hedging loss is a one-time print but the equity-multiple compression is the crisis-unwind signal.** Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser line carries forward (2-5 ships/day, 100M bbl/week lost). Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **B4 TTF €46.63 + Container freight FBX13 stable = confirms quiet European-energy-channel.** PRIOR (May 13): TTF €46.23/MWh May 11 (+4.74% 1D, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline, +31.57% YoY) — stepped up on Trump rejection + Aramco 2-5 ships/day Hormuz deterioration. **Cheniere LNG $240.70 May 12 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M, +10.8% 3M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM** still in price; CCO Anatol Feygin (May 7): 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players are SHORT the war via hedge books. **Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: 2-5 ships/day (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June.** Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **NEW VARIABLE:** WSJ May 11 revealed UAE secretly struck Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery April 8 (55,000 bpd offline months) — affects oil specifically, not LNG, but reframes Gulf-state covert military participation. Helium node update: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion. **Reversibility:** TTF/HH partial decoupling continues; Cheniere -9.1% 1M reflects markets pricing-through peace optionality despite operational tightness. Qatar Ras Laffan reopening is in any MOU framework but physical recovery years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.

current.delta_1m: HH +3.3%; TTF -25%+ from March peak; Cheniere -9.7%

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — TTF ~€44/MWh weekly (-25%+ from March €60+ peak, +40%+ vs Feb ~€31 baseline); Cheniere $240.11 May 8 close (-2.7% 1D, -9.7% 1M, +11.3% 3M); Q1 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term hedges struck pre-war at lower TTF/HH spread became deep underwater after Qatar force majeure spiked TTF €60+ in March). Cheniere CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance RAISED to $7.25-7.75B (from $6.75-7.25B). HEADLINE GAAP LOSS IS NON-CASH; CASH EARNINGS POWER INCREASED. **Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike is operationally adjacent to Ras Laffan — Qatar's main LNG/helium export complex.** If strike location was deliberate, signals graduated escalation against Qatari LNG export infrastructure rather than against US-blockade-facilitating shipping. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. Cheniere is cleanest US-LNG-exporter beneficiary of any extended Qatar disruption.
sources: Bloomberg (Cheniere CCO comment + Q1 derivative loss)
UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED prices yfinance multi-source; Cheniere Q1 derivative loss = CONFIRMED Bloomberg primary; Cheniere CCO Hormuz comment = CONFIRMED public; bulk carrier strike near Ras Laffan = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary); deliberate-vs-coincidental strike location = OPEN question

recent_signals.headline: BRANCH 3 → CHENIERE EUROPEAN-ENERGY-EMERGENCY PREMIUM COMPRESSION; HH +6.8% NON-CRISIS

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan (world's largest LNG facility, ~20% of global supply) after attacks | Iranian missiles damaged LNG Trains 4 & 6 at Ras Laffan | European TTF surged 70% in March to EUR54
